content.claim: The EU suspended €32 billion in funding to Hungary but released €10.2 billion in cohesion funds in December 2023 following judicial reforms. Multiple observers criticized the release decision as politically transactional, coinciding with Budapest's withdrawal of a veto on Ukraine aid, and the Commission has not deployed sector-specific SEN/education conditionality despite the open infringement procedure.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The causal link between geopolitical bargaining and fund releases is inferred rather than formally confirmed by the Commission
The absence of sector-specific conditioning on SEN/segregation does not rule out future application, only reflects past practice
EU financial leverage over Hungary on this dossier remains formally available but empirically underutilized
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
